rulings.Case Title: JIM D PARRISH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate Actions For All Purposes
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion to consolidate actions 274209 and 275150 for all purposes: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD L. BASS et al VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiff¿s motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CASEY, PATRICIA AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Wrongful Death Proceedings;
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion to stay wrongful death proceedings is granted. Plaintiffs do not oppose a stay. The wrongful death action is stayed until Remittitur to this court has been issued by the Court of Appeal, upon which plaintiffs must notify the court and the stay will be lifted. Any new trial date will be set within 60 days following the filing of the Remittitur with this court. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CASEY, PATRICIA AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'S Motion To Stay Wrongful Death Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿s joinder in Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion to stay wrongful death proceedings is off calendar as moot given the ruling on the underlying motion. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL TODD VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Puget Sound Commerce Center, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that Campbell v. Ford Motor Company (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th15 bars plaintiff¿s claim. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiff produced evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant knew or reasonably should have known that the installation, removal and/or disturbance of asbestos-containing material could create a safety hazard of secondary exposure for a member of a household of an employee. Pursuant to Rowland v. Christian (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108 and Campbell v. Ford Motor Company (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th15, public policy dictates for the creation of a duty in the present matter. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
